title: Crunchy Cured Creamy Mediterranean Eggplant Gratin
description: A rich and savory eggplant gratin with crunchy cured cheese crust, creamy bechamel sauce, and a hint of Mediterranean herbs.

ingredients.name: unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: plus more for greasing the baking dish

instructions: Cut the eggplants in half lengthwise and place them cut-side up on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle the eggplants with olive oil and season with salt and pepper. Roast in the oven for 30 minutes, or until tender and lightly browned.
Sauté the chopped parsley, oregano, and thyme in butter until fragrant.
In a small saucepan, melt the butter over low heat. Add the flour and whisk to make a roux, cooking for 1-2 minutes.
Slowly pour in the bechamel sauce, whisking continuously to avoid lumps. Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until thickened.
In a greased 9x13-inch baking dish, create a layer of eggplant slices.
Spread half of the bechamel sauce over the eggplant, followed by half of the grated Gruyère cheese.
Repeat the layers, starting with the eggplant, then the bechamel sauce, and finishing with the remaining Gruyère cheese.
Sprinkle the crumbled feta cheese over the top of the gratin and bake for an additional 15-20 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the gratin from the oven and let it cool for 10-15 minutes before serving.
